name: Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago
mission: Teaching and reading of the holy Quran, daily and Friday congregations, Islamic Education for Children and perform marriages and funeral services
theoryOfChange: By providing a combination of daily worship, full-time and part-time Islamic education, and social services (Zakat/Clinic), the organization aims to develop moral and responsible leaders and maintain economic balance in the Muslim community.
scoreSummary: Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago shows good impact and moderate alignment, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Donate your ZAKAT to ISNS. Mail your zakat donation check to the following address: 3950 Industrial Avenue Rolling Meadows, IL 60008, USA. Drop it off in the Zakah drop box in the Masjid. Donate your Zakat online and ISNS shall distribute it to the needy Muslims in our local community. (Source: https://isns.org)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

all_citations.claim: The organization provides Quranic teaching, daily prayers, and religious services while maintaining a 92.5% program expense ratio.

The organization is Zakat-eligible and offers various giving methods for religious and community support.

dimension_explanations.alignment: ISNS shows a high donor fit for those seeking to support local religious infrastructure and is designated as Zakat-eligible [3]. The organization addresses the urgent religious and cultural needs of the Northwest Suburbs through its Sunday School and specialized life-cycle services [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The society focuses on capacity building within the Muslim community, maintaining a high program expense ratio of 92.5% to fund its daily prayers and educational initiatives [1][2]. With a total revenue of $3,697,122 and 117.3 months of working capital, the organization possesses significant financial stability to sustain its mission [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago serves as a vital community hub providing comprehensive religious education and essential spiritual services.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 92.5% ensures the vast majority of funds directly support community services
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.01 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity position with 117.3 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago (ISNS) is a religious non-profit dedicated to the teaching of the Holy Quran and the facilitation of daily and Friday congregational prayers [1]. The organization manages a robust suite of programs including a Sunday School for youth and the performance of Islamic marriage and funeral services for the local community [1][2].

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="6">92.5% program expense ratio</cite> and spends only <cite id="7">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite>, maximizing the impact of every donation.
With a history <cite id="1">dating back to 1974</cite>, the society has over 50 years of experience in <cite id="8">community service and religious education</cite>.
The society holds <cite id="12">117.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing an extraordinary level of financial security for long-term operations.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago stands out for its lean operational model. According to its <cite id="2">2023 financial data</cite>, the organization successfully scaled its revenue to over $4 million while keeping administrative costs negligible. This is particularly evident in its <cite id="13">92.5% independent board</cite>, which provides oversight without the overhead typical of larger national charities. Furthermore, its <cite id="14">total assets of $10,997,666</cite> against minimal liabilities of $79,635 demonstrate a fortress-like balance sheet that supports its <cite id="5">full-time school and hifz programs</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Society of Northwest Suburbs of Chicago (ISNS) serves as a cornerstone for the Muslim community in Rolling Meadows, Illinois, providing essential religious, educational, and social services. <cite id="1">founded in 1974</cite>, the organization has grown into a significant institution that manages <cite id="2">$4,092,615 in annual revenue</cite> while maintaining a primary focus on the <cite id="3">teaching and reading of the Holy Quran</cite>. Its operations encompass a wide range of community needs, from <cite id="4">daily and Friday congregational prayers</cite> to specialized youth programming through its <cite id="5">Sunday School and Al Safa Academy</cite>. Financially, the organization demonstrates a unique model of efficiency, reporting a <cite id="6">92.5% program expense ratio</cite> in its most recent filings, ensuring that nearly all contributions directly fund its mission-critical services. This fiscal discipline is further evidenced by a <cite id="7">fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that the society relies heavily on community-driven support rather than expensive marketing campaigns. Beyond education, ISNS provides vital life-cycle services, including <cite id="8">performing marriages and funeral services</cite> for the local population. The organization's growth is notable, achieving a <cite id="9">3-year revenue CAGR of 39.9%</cite>, which reflects increasing community trust and expanding service capacity. As a <cite id="10">zakat-eligible entity</cite>, it serves as a reliable vehicle for donors to fulfill their religious obligations, specifically targeting the <cite id="11">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> within its service area.
